raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The federal Crime Victims Fund is experiencing a decline in funding, projected to be 41 percent lower nationwide in fiscal year 2024 compared to fiscal year 2023.",
    "California has not updated its criminal code to account for the size and power of corporations.",
    "Assembly Bill 2432 would create a new state-level funding mechanism for crime victim service programs.",
    "AB 2432 proposes to increase the maximum criminal fines for corporations to generally twice the amount taken from victims or twice the loss caused by the defendant.",
    "Under current law, the maximum criminal fines imposed on corporations are generally $10,000 per felony.",
    "Restitution fines for corporations would be increased to $100,000 from $10,000 under AB 2432.",
    "AB 2432 would help ensure corporate offenders are held accountable and serve as a deterrent for corporate crime in California.",
    "The misconduct by Plains All American Pipeline, L.P. during the 2015 Refugio Oil Spill is estimated to have caused over $200 million in damages."
  ]
}
```
